metadata.summary: Structured API error responses using RFC 7807 Problem Details with type URIs, traceIds for log correlation, and field-level validation errors via shared Pydantic handlers.

content.title: API Error Handling
content.summary: Inconsistent error shapes force clients to write per-endpoint error parsing. RFC 7807 is an IETF standard that gives clients a predictable envelope to inspect without reading docs. The traceId field correlates client-reported errors to server logs, cutting debugging time dramatically. Never exposing raw exception messages prevents information leakage.

why.p: Inconsistent error shapes force clients to write per-endpoint error parsing. RFC 7807 is an IETF standard that gives clients a predictable envelope to inspect without reading docs. The traceId field correlates client-reported errors to server logs, cutting debugging time dramatically. Never exposing raw exception messages prevents information leakage.

list.item: Designing a new REST or HTTP API from scratch
Standardising error responses across an existing API with inconsistent formats
Adding structured field-level validation errors for form-backing endpoints
When debugging requires correlating client errors to server-side log traces
GraphQL APIs — use the errors array in the GraphQL envelope instead
Internal service-to-service APIs where both sides are controlled and a simpler format is agreed — RFC 7807 overhead is not always justified
WebSocket or streaming APIs where error framing is protocol-specific

section.name: problem-detail-schema
section.p: The RFC 7807 error envelope, required/optional fields, error code catalogue, and message quality rules. Every error response must include type (URI), title (short summary), and status (HTTP code). Include detail (human explanation), instance (request path), traceId (for log correlation), and errors array (for field-level validation). The type URI is machine-readable and can link to documentation. The traceId enables client-to-server log correlation without exposing internal details. The errors array gives field-level feedback for form-backing endpoints.

rule.statement: Every error response must include type (URI), title (short summary), and status (HTTP code). Include detail (human explanation), instance (request path), traceId (for log correlation), and errors array (for field-level validation).
rule.rationale: The type URI is machine-readable and can link to documentation. The traceId enables client-to-server log correlation without exposing internal details. The errors array gives field-level feedback for form-backing endpoints.

example.note: RFC 7807 validation error response with field-level errors array.
example.code: {"type":"https://api.example.com/errors/validation-error","title":"Validation Error","status":400,"detail":"The request body contains invalid fields","instance":"/users/create","traceId":"abc-123-xyz","errors":[{"field":"email","code":"invalid_format","message":"Must be a valid email address"},{"field":"age","code":"out_of_range","message":"Must be between 18 and 120"}]}

antipattern.description: Returning raw exception messages or stack traces in error responses.
antipattern.reason: Exposes internal implementation details; violates information security; confuses API consumers expecting a stable error contract.

section.name: error-code-catalogue
section.p: Use machine-readable snake_case error codes in the code field. Define all error codes as constants in a single module. Error codes enable clients to programmatically branch logic; human-readable messages can change without breaking client code that branches on code.

rule.statement: Use machine-readable snake_case error codes in the code field (not HTTP status descriptions). Define all error codes as constants in a single module.
rule.rationale: Error codes enable clients to programmatically branch logic; human-readable messages can change without breaking client code that branches on code.

list.item: Client (4xx): validation_error, invalid_format, missing_field, unauthorized, forbidden, not_found, conflict, rate_limited

rule.statement: Define a single ProblemDetail Pydantic model shared across all exception handlers. Never construct error dicts inline in handlers.
rule.rationale: A shared model enforces schema consistency across all error paths and enables OpenAPI schema generation for the error response type.

example.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel
class ProblemDetail(BaseModel):
 type: str
 title: str
 status: int
 detail: str | None = None
 instance: str | None = None
 trace_id: str | None = None
 errors: list[dict] | None = None

template.purpose: ProblemDetail Pydantic model + FastAPI exception handler wiring
ErrorCode constants for client and server error categories
